title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
EPFO Pension Fund Withdrawal
Saving for life after retirement is a crucial aspect of long-term financial stability, especially for salaried employees. The Employees’ Provident Fund (EPF) helps achieve this by encouraging regular savings through mandatory contributions during employment.
But EPFO pension fund withdrawal is often misunderstood; many salaried employees assume their pension can be easily accessed whenever they want, but in reality, pension withdrawals are governed by rigid eligibility rules and specific criteria.
If you are nearing your retirement and want to know the PF withdrawal rules or the tax on PF withdrawal, you are in the right place, because in this blog, we will explain everything, right from how to calculate EPFO pension withdrawal amount to the process of PF pension withdrawal.
What Is the Employee Pension Scheme EPS?
The Employee Pension Scheme, called EPS in short, was launched in the year 1995 under the aegis of the Employees' Provident Fund Organisation to provide salaried employees with a reliable pension after their retirement. The scheme applies to employees working in organisations covered under the EPF Act and is designed to ensure long-term income security of the employees working in the organisation.
Under the EPS scheme, both you and your employer each put 12% of your salary into your EPF account. From your employer's contribution, 8.33% (up to ₹15,000) goes to the EPS, and the rest goes to the EPF. If you earn less than ₹15,000 a month, it's mandatory for you to have an EPF account.
An employee's monthly pension under the Employees’ Pension Scheme (EPS) is based on their pensionable salary and how long they've contributed. These go into a standard EPS formula to figure out the amount of pension.
How is the EPS Pension Calculated?
The monthly pension is calculated as: (Pensionable Salary × Pensionable Service) ÷ 70
This is the same for everyone who qualifies for EPS.
Pensionable Salary
Pensionable salary is the average monthly salary from the last five years (60 months) before leaving the Employees’ Pension Scheme. The Supreme Court made this decision on November 4, 2022, changing the old rule.
Before, the pensionable salary was figured out using the average salary from the last year (12 months). Now, the average of the last five years is used to determine the amount of pension.
Pensionable Service
Pensionable service is the total number of years for which contributions were made to the EPS account. According to EPS rules, the service period has to be rounded to the closest full year. If someone has worked for six months or more, it’s rounded up to a full year. If it’s less than six months, it’s not counted.
Also, if an employee retires at 58 and has worked for over 20 years, two extra years are added to their pensionable service when calculating the pension.
Steps to Withdraw the Pension Contribution Through the Online Mode
The online process is simple, but you'll need to link your Aadhaar card with your UAN first.
Step 1: Go to the Unified Member Sewa portal and log in using your UAN and password.
Step 2: Click on ‘Online Services’ then ‘Claim (Form-31, 19, 10C & 10D)’.
Step 3: Double-check your member and KYC details. Type in your bank’s account number and then click on ‘Verify’.
Step 4: For claim type, select ‘Withdraw Pension Only’.
Step 5: Where it says ‘I want to apply for’, pick ‘Only Pension Withdrawal (Form 10C)’.
Step 6: Type in your permanent address, check the disclaimer box, and click ‘Get Aadhaar OTP’.
Step 7: Type in the OTP you get on your Aadhaar-linked mobile, validate it, and click ‘Submit Claim Form’.
Step 8: You'll get a confirmation SMS. Once your claim is approved, the EPS amount will be sent to your bank account.
After you submit Form 10C, you'll get an SMS on your registered mobile number. Your pension claim, along with Form 10C, will be submitted, and the EPS pension amount will be transferred to your bank account.
Steps to Withdraw Pension Contribution Offline
Step 1: First, get the claim form (either the Aadhaar or non-Aadhaar one) from the EPFO website.
Step 2:If you're using the Aadhaar version, double-check that your Aadhaar is linked to your bank account.
Step 3:Next, fill the form with your bank info and personal details. Make sure everything is correct.
Step 4:Finally, send the filled-out form to your local EPF office so they can process it.
Track Your Status of EPFO Pension Withdrawal Online
There are 3 ways you can track your financial status online. Keep in mind that withdrawing from your EPFO might affect your income tax, depending on your tax liability
Check your PF claim on the EPFO website:
Step 1: Head to the EPFO website, find ‘Services,’ and then click ‘For Employees.’
Step 2: Select ‘Know the Claim Status.’
Step 3: You’ll be sent to the member passbook area.
Step 4: Enter your UAN, password, and the captcha to log in.
Step 5: Click ‘View Claim Status’ to see how your claim is coming along.
Also, EPFO sends SMS updates to your linked mobile number when they receive your claim and when the money hits your bank account.
Check your PF claim status on the UAN member portal:
Step 1: Sign in to the UAN Member Portal with your UAN and password.
Step 2: Find the ‘Online Services’ tab and click ‘Track Claim Status.’
Step 3: You’ll see all the details about the claim status on the screen.
If you applied offline, you won’t be able to see your status here, but you can still check it on the EPFO website.
Check your PF claim status on the  UMANG App.
The UMANG app (available for both iOS and Android) lets you track your PF claims. Just make sure your mobile number is linked to your UAN. If it is, you can skip re-entering it in these steps:
Step 1: Get the UMANG app from the Play Store or App Store.
Step 2: Log in to your EPFO account with your mobile number, using either MPIN or OTP.
Step 3: Once you’re in, go to ‘Employee Centric Services.’
Step 4: You’ll be taken to the EPFO portal main page again. Here, pick ‘Employee Centric Services.’
Step 5: Hit ‘Track Claim’ to see the status of your EPF claim.
Step 6: Your claim details will show up. If you don’t have any active claims, the screen will be blank.
Documents Required for EPFO Pension Fund Withdrawal
To comply with the PF pension withdrawal rules, you need to present the following documents:
Address proof
Bank account statement
2 revenue stamps
Proof of identity
What Is the Withdrawal Limit?
Complete Withdrawal
You can only take out your entire EPF savings if you're unemployed or retired. If you lose your job, you can get 75% of your PF money right away, and the other 25% after a year.
You need to wait 36 months after losing your job to withdraw your pension. To get all of your PF money, you have to be out of work for at least two months and not transitioning between jobs.
There are also a few special situations where you can withdraw the full amount if you have worked for at least 1 year, i.e. 12 months.
If your workplace closes for more than 15 days, employees are jobless without pay.
If you haven't received your salary for over two months straight.
You can withdraw 100% of your share with interest, but the reason for non-payment could be anything other than a strike.
Partial Withdrawal
Partial EPF withdrawal is allowed only for specific purposes, and each purpose has defined limits and service requirements.
Eligibility Criteria for Withdrawal
To get your pension under the EPF Act, there's a set process you need to follow after retirement. But you can claim it even before retirement if you meet these specific conditions.
You can start getting your pension at the age of 50 or later. If you've worked for at least 10 years, you can even withdraw earlier in case of emergency, but keep in mind that it will reduce your pension amount. It goes down by 4% each year until you hit 50.
If you've worked for over six months but less than ten years, you can still take out your pension money in case you are jobless for two months.
If you retire at 58 but haven't worked the required 10 years, you won't get a monthly pension. But you can still take out the full amount from your EPS account at once.
Eligibility to Withdraw EPF Completely After Retirement
An employee can withdraw their entire EPF balance after retirement. According to the EPFO, the age of retirement is 55 years. However, withdrawal of up to 90% of the EPF balance is allowed one year before retiring, provided the member has completed 54 years of age.
Mandatory Requirements for UAN and KYC
To keep your Provident Fund (PF) account secure, the EPFO asks all members to finish their Know Your Customer (KYC) process. This confirms your identity by connecting your Aadhaar, PAN, and bank info with your Universal Account Number (UAN). Doing this lets you use all EPF services online. For many EPF processes, Aadhaar-based KYC is a must.
Once your KYC is done, you can withdraw and move your PF money online without needing your employer to get involved. Claims get handled more quickly, and taxes are deducted accurately. The EPFO sends updates to you promptly. Claims are often turned down or delayed if KYC details are missing or not confirmed.
PF withdrawal rules
There are certain rules regarding the PF pension fund withdrawal, some of which are listed below.
Tax Implications of Early PF Withdrawals
Employees’ Provident Fund withdrawals are allowed in situations such as retirement, unemployment, or specific personal needs, including medical treatment, marriage, education, and housing. While employees can withdraw EPF fully or partially, the tax impact depends on the length of continuous service and the timing of the withdrawal. Withdrawals made after completing five years of continuous service are fully exempt from tax, whereas early withdrawals may be taxable, even though no TDS is deducted if the amount is below ₹50,000. While the employee’s contribution is not taxed, interest earned and the employer’s contribution become taxable if the five-year condition is not met, making timing an important factor.
Loan Facility
You can withdraw up to 90% of your EPF contribution if you want to repay an existing home loan.
Withdrawal Restrictions While Employed
If you are currently employed, you cannot withdraw your entire EPF balance. EPFO permits partial access to funds only for specific needs such as medical emergencies, education, marriage, or housing loans, etc., but you also must fulfil certain service year requirements before making a claim. These restrictions are designed to protect your retirement savings while still offering limited flexibility when needed.
EPF Transfer on Job Change
When switching jobs, employees can either withdraw their PF balance or transfer it to the new employer’s account, but transferring is usually preferred by most of the employees. EPF transfer can be done online through the UAN unified portal, provided your UAN is activated, Aadhaar and bank details are linked, e-KYC is approved by the employer, and both old and new PF account details are correctly reflected in EPFO records.
Unlike withdrawal, transferring PF does not attract any tax or TDS and helps maintain continuity of service, which is important for claiming full tax exemption on future EPF withdrawals after five years. In addition, transferring the balance allows your retirement corpus to continue earning compound interest, making it more beneficial than withdrawing PF during a job transition.
Benefits of EPFO Pension Withdrawal
EPFO pension withdrawal can offer immediate financial relief during periods of unemployment or career transitions. It helps individuals access accumulated pension benefits when long-term continuation is not feasible.
Faster Processing Time
Online EPF withdrawal has made the claim process significantly faster by removing paperwork and reducing repeated reliance on employer approvals for KYC-compliant UANs. Claims filed through the EPFO portal are verified and tracked digitally, leading to quicker settlements. EPFO is also working on enabling withdrawals through UPI using the BHIM app, where members are expected to view their total balance, eligible withdrawal amount, and the mandatory 25% minimum balance, with an initial transaction cap of ₹25,000.
Simple Withdrawal Process
The EPFO has streamlined the PF withdrawal process to make access to funds quicker and easier. Eligible members can now withdraw up to 75% of their PF balance at any time without furnishing any documents, using the online portal. To protect the long-term retirement corpus, EPFO mandates that 25% of the PF balance remain in the account at any time.
Anytime Anywhere Access
Earlier, the process of withdrawal was complex, and it took several days for employers to actually receive the money. But now, with the integration of ATM and UPI in EPF 3.0, employees will be able to withdraw their PF money anywhere and anytime they want
Real Time Transaction Updates
Real-time transaction updates ensure that members remain informed throughout the EPF withdrawal or transfer process. From claim submission to final settlement, updates are reflected online and through alerts, offering better control and visibility over PF transactions.
Improved User Experience
EPFO’s digital interface has significantly improved the overall user experience by simplifying the process. Members can now manage withdrawals, transfers, and claim tracking with just a few clicks anywhere, anytime.
Key Takeaways
EPFO pension withdrawal is governed by strict eligibility rules based on age, years of service, and employment status, and cannot be accessed freely at any time.
The Employees’ Pension Scheme (EPS) pension amount is calculated using a standard formula that considers pensionable salary (average of last 5 years) and pensionable service.
Online pension withdrawal has simplified the process, provided Aadhaar is linked with UAN and KYC is complete.
Tax implications depend on years of continuous service, with withdrawals after five years generally being tax-exempt and early withdrawals potentially taxable.
Partial withdrawals and advance options are allowed only for specific purposes and under defined conditions to protect long-term retirement savings.
While EPFO pension provides post-retirement income support, having life insurance is important to ensure the overall protection of dependents
Conclusion
EPFO’s ongoing reforms have made managing provident fund accounts simpler and better for employees at every career stage. Whether it is withdrawing funds, transferring PF on a job change, or tracking transactions in real time, members now benefit from a more streamlined experience. While EPF remains a strong foundation for retirement planning, it may not be sufficient on its own to cover long-term protection and fulfil emergency needs. Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance can help shield your family from the financial shocks of unfortunate events.
FAQs
Can I withdraw my pension fund when I resign?
Yes, you withdraw pension fund after resignation, but only if your total service is less than 10 years and you remain unemployed for at least two months without joining another job, you are eligible to withdraw your EPFO pension fund.
How does EPF differ from life insurance?
EPF focuses on long-term retirement savings through regular contributions, while life insurance, in exchange for premium aim to provide financial security to the dependents in case of the member’s death
Can I withdraw 100% of my pension fund?
Full PF withdrawal is allowed only on retirement or an extensive unemployment period. After becoming unemployed, 75% can be withdrawn in the first month. The balance 25% can only be withdrawn after 12 months of unemployment, and pension withdrawal is allowed only after 36 months.
What is Form 10C for PF withdrawal?
Form 10C is used to claim withdrawal of the EPF pension contribution or to obtain a scheme certificate when an employee exits service before becoming eligible for a monthly pension under the Employees’ Pension Scheme (EPS).
Disclaimer
The content provided is for general informational purposes only. The material is compiled from publicly available sources, internal insights, and other information deemed reliable. While reasonable care has been taken in compiling the information, Bajaj Life Insurance Limited (Formerly known as Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance Company Limited) assumes no liability for its accuracy. The opinions expressed do not constitute formal recommendations, professional advice, or definitive guidance. Readers are encouraged to conduct their own due diligence and are advised to seek independent professional or expert advice before making any financial or investment decisions based on the content. Any illustrations included are for conceptual clarity only and do not reflect the actual performance of any product or offering.

E Pension Scheme Withdrawal
Saving for life after retirement is a crucial aspect of long-term financial stability, especially for salaried employees. The Employees’ Provident Fund (EPF) helps achieve this by encouraging regular savings through mandatory contributions during employment.
But EPFO pension fund withdrawal is often misunderstood; many salaried employees might think that premature withdrawals are allowed. However, in reality, pension withdrawals are governed by specific eligibility rules and criteria.
If you are nearing your retirement and want to know the PF withdrawal rules or the tax on PF withdrawal, you are in the right place, because in this blog, we will explain everything, right from how to calculate EPFO pension withdrawal amount to the process of PF pension withdrawal.
What Is the Employee Pension Scheme EPS?
The Employee Pension Scheme, called EPS in short, was launched in the year 1995 under the aegis of the Employees' Provident Fund Organisation to provide salaried employees with a reliable pension after their retirement1. The scheme applies to employees working in organisations covered under the EPF Act and is designed to ensure long-term income security of the employees working in the organisation.
Under the EPS scheme, both you and your employer each put 12% of your salary into your EPF account. From your employer's contribution, 8.33% (up to ₹15,000) goes to the EPS, and the rest goes to the EPF1. If you earn less than ₹15,000 a month, it's mandatory for you to have an EPF account1.
An employee's monthly pension under the Employees’ Pension Scheme (EPS) is based on their pensionable salary and how long they've contributed. These go into a standard EPS formula to figure out the amount of pension.
How is the EPS Pension Calculated?
The monthly pension is calculated as: (Pensionable Salary × Pensionable Service) ÷ 702
1. Pensionable Salary
Pensionable salary is the average monthly salary from the last five years (60 months) before leaving the Employees’ Pension Scheme2.
Previously, the pensionable salary was figured out using the average salary from the last year (12 months). Now, the average of the last five years is used to determine the amount of pension2.
2. Pensionable Service
Pensionable service is the total number of years for which contributions were made to the EPS account. According to EPS rules, the service period has to be rounded to the closest full year. If someone has worked for six months or more, it’s rounded up to a full year. If it’s less than six months, it’s not counted.
Also, if an employee retires at 58 and has worked for over 20 years, two extra years are added to their pensionable service when calculating the pension2.
Steps to Withdraw the Pension Contribution Through the Online Mode
The online process is simple, but you'll need to link your Aadhaar card with your UAN first1.
Step 1: Go to the Unified Member Sewa portal and log in using your UAN and password.
Step 2: Click on ‘Online Services’ then ‘Claim (Form-31, 19, 10C & 10D)’.
Step 3: Double-check your member and KYC details. Type in your bank’s account number and then click on ‘Verify’.
Step 4: For claim type, select ‘Withdraw Pension Only’.
Step 5: Where it says ‘I want to apply for’, pick ‘Only Pension Withdrawal (Form 10C)’.
Step 6: Type in your permanent address, check the disclaimer box, and click ‘Get Aadhaar OTP’.
Step 7: Type in the OTP you get on your Aadhaar-linked mobile, validate it, and click ‘Submit Claim Form’.
Step 8: You'll get a confirmation SMS. Once your claim is approved, the EPS amount will be sent to your bank account.
After you submit Form 10C, you'll get an SMS on your registered mobile number. Your pension claim, along with Form 10C, will be submitted, and the EPS pension amount will be transferred to your bank account.
Steps to Withdraw Pension Contribution Offline1
Step 1: First, get the claim form (either the Aadhaar or non-Aadhaar one) from the EPFO website.
Step 2:If you're using the Aadhaar version, double-check that your Aadhaar is linked to your bank account.
Step 3:Next, fill the form with your bank info and personal details. Make sure everything is correct.
Step 4:Finally, send the filled-out form to your local EPF office so they can process it.
Track Your Status of EPFO Pension Withdrawal Online
There are 3 ways you can track your financial status online. Keep in mind that withdrawing from your EPFO might affect your income tax, depending on your tax liability
1. Check your PF claim on the EPFO website:
● Step 1: Head to the EPFO website, find ‘Services,’ and then click ‘For Employees.’ ● Step 2: Select ‘Know the Claim Status.’ ● Step 3: You’ll be sent to the member passbook area. ● Step 4: Enter your UAN, password, and the captcha to log in. ● Step 5: Click ‘View Claim Status’ to see how your claim is coming along.
Also, EPFO sends SMS updates to your linked mobile number when they receive your claim and when the money hits your bank account.
2. Check your PF claim status on the UAN member portal:
● Step 1: Sign in to the UAN Member Portal with your UAN and password. ● Step 2: Find the ‘Online Services’ tab and click ‘Track Claim Status.’ ● Step 3: You’ll see all the details about the claim status on the screen.
If you applied offline, you won’t be able to see your status here, but you can still check it on the EPFO website.
3. Check your PF claim status on the UMANG App.
The UMANG app (available for both iOS and Android) lets you track your PF claims. Just make sure your mobile number is linked to your UAN. If it is, you can skip re-entering it in these steps:
● Step 1: Get the UMANG app from the Play Store or App Store. ● Step 2: Log in to your EPFO account with your mobile number, using either MPIN or OTP. ● Step 3: Once you’re in, go to ‘Employee Centric Services.’ ● Step 4: You’ll be taken to the EPFO portal main page again. Here, pick ‘Employee Centric Services.’ ● Step 5: Hit ‘Track Claim’ to see the status of your EPF claim. ● Step 6: Your claim details will show up. If you don’t have any active claims, the screen will be blank.
Documents Required for EPFO Pension Fund Withdrawal
To comply with the PF pension withdrawal rules, you need to present the following documents:
1. Address proof1 2. Bank account statement1 3. 2 revenue stamps1 4. Proof of identity1
What Is the Withdrawal Limit?
Complete Withdrawal
You can only take out your entire EPF savings if you're unemployed or retired. If you lose your job, you can get 75% of your PF money right away, and the other 25% after a year3.
You need to wait 36 months after losing your job to withdraw your pension. To get all of your PF money, you have to be out of work for at least two months and not transitioning between jobs.
Partial Withdrawal
Partial EPF withdrawal is allowed only for specific purposes, and each purpose has defined limits and service requirements4.

Eligibility Criteria for Withdrawal
To get your pension under EPS , there's a set process you need to follow after retirement. But you can claim it even before retirement if you meet these specific conditions.
1. You can start getting your pension at the age of 50 or later and have worked at least 10
years, you can even get pension early in case of emergency, but keep in mind that it will reduce your pension amount. It goes down by 4% each year until you hit 50. 2. If you've worked for over six months but less than ten years and have been unemployed for
2 months or more, you can withdraw your pension contribution. 3. If you retire at 58 but haven't worked for the required 10 years, you won't get a monthly
pension. But you can still take out the full amount from your EPS account at once.
Eligibility to Withdraw EPF Completely After Retirement5
An employee can withdraw their entire EPF balance after retirement. According to the EPFO, the age of retirement is 55 years. However, withdrawal of up to 90% of the EPF balance is allowed one year before retiring, provided the member has completed 54 years of age.
Mandatory Requirements for UAN and KYC
To keep your Provident Fund (PF) account secure, the EPFO asks all members to finish their Know Your Customer (KYC) process. This confirms your identity by connecting your Aadhaar, PAN, and bank info with your Universal Account Number (UAN). Doing this lets you use all EPF services online. For many EPF processes, Aadhaar-based KYC is a must.
Once your KYC is done, you can withdraw your PF money online without much hassle5 . Claims get handled more quickly, and taxes are deducted accurately. The EPFO sends updates to you promptly.
PF withdrawal rules
There are certain rules regarding the PF pension fund withdrawal, some of which are listed below.
1. Tax Implications of Early PF Withdrawals
Employees’ Provident Fund withdrawals are allowed in situations such as retirement, unemployment, or specific personal needs, including medical treatment, marriage, education, and housing. While employees can withdraw EPF fully or partially, the tax impact depends on the length of continuous service and the timing of the withdrawal. Withdrawals made after completing five years of continuous service are fully exempt from tax, whereas early withdrawals may be taxable, even though no TDS is deducted if the amount is below ₹50,000. While the employee’s contribution is not taxed, interest earned and the employer’s contribution become taxable if the five-year condition is not met, making timing an important factor.
2. Loan Facility
You can withdraw up to 90% of your EPF contribution if you want to repay an existing home loan subject to the conditions prescribed by EPFO.
3. Withdrawal Restrictions While Employed
If you are currently employed, you cannot withdraw your entire EPF balance. EPFO permits partial withdrawals or advances only for specific needs such as medical emergencies, education, marriage, or housing loans, etc., but you also must fulfil certain service year requirements before making a claim. These restrictions are designed to protect your retirement savings while still offering limited flexibility when needed.
4. EPF Transfer on Job Change
When switching jobs, employees can either withdraw their PF balance or transfer it to the new employer’s account, but transferring is usually preferred by most of the employees. EPF transfer can be done online through the UAN unified portal, provided your UAN is activated, Aadhaar and bank details are linked, e-KYC is approved by the employer, and both old and new PF account details are correctly reflected in EPFO records.
Unlike withdrawal, transferring PF does not attract any tax or TDS and helps maintain continuity of service, which is important for claiming full tax exemption on future EPF
withdrawals after five years. In addition, transferring the balance allows your retirement corpus to continue earning compound interest, making it more beneficial than withdrawing PF during a job transition.
Benefits of EPFO Pension Withdrawal
EPFO pension withdrawal can offer immediate financial relief during periods of unemployment or career transitions. It helps individuals access accumulated pension benefits when long-term continuation is not feasible.
1. Faster Processing Time
Online EPF withdrawal has made the claim process significantly faster . Claims filed through the EPFO portal are verified and tracked digitally, leading to quicker settlements. EPFO is also working on enabling withdrawals through UPI using the BHIM app, where members are expected to view their total balance, eligible withdrawal amount, and the mandatory 25% minimum balance, with an initial transaction cap of ₹25,0006.
2. Simple Withdrawal Process
The EPFO has streamlined the PF withdrawal process to make access to funds quicker and easier. EPF members can now withdraw up to 75% of their PF balance at any time without furnishing any documents, using the online portal7. To protect the long-term retirement corpus, EPFO mandates that 25% of the PF balance remain in the account at any time7.
3. Anytime Anywhere Access7
With the rollout of EPF 3.0, the integration of ATM and UPI is expected to significantly improve access to provident fund withdrawals. Once implemented, employees will be able to withdraw their PF money instantly, offering employees easier and faster access to their PF money through ATMs and UPI, once the feature becomes operational. This, however, is subject to specific terms and conditions.
4. Real Time Transaction Updates
Real-time transaction updates ensure that members remain informed throughout the EPF withdrawal or transfer process. From claim submission to final settlement, updates are reflected online and through alerts, offering better control and visibility over PF transactions.
5. Improved User Experience
EPFO’s digital interface has significantly improved the overall user experience by simplifying the process. Members can now manage withdrawals, transfers, and claim tracking with just a few clicks .
Key Takeaways
1. EPFO pension withdrawal is governed by strict eligibility rules based on age, years of service,
and employment status, and cannot be accessed freely at any time.
2. The Employees’ Pension Scheme (EPS) pension amount is calculated using a standard
formula that considers pensionable salary (average of last 5 years2) and pensionable service. 3. Online pension withdrawal has simplified the process, provided Aadhaar is linked with UAN
and KYC is complete. 4. Tax implications depend on years of continuous service, with withdrawals after five years
generally being tax-exempt and early withdrawals potentially taxable3. 5. Partial withdrawals and advance options are allowed only for specific purposes and under
defined conditions to protect long-term retirement savings. 6. While EPFO pension provides post-retirement income support, having life insurance is
important to ensure the overall financial protection of dependents
Conclusion
EPFO’s ongoing reforms have made managing provident fund accounts simpler and better for employees at every career stage. Whether it is withdrawing funds, transferring PF on a job change, or tracking transactions in real time, members now benefit from a more streamlined experience. While EPF remains a strong foundation for retirement planning, it may not be sufficient on its own to cover long-term protection and fulfil emergency needs.
FAQs
1. Can I withdraw my pension fund when I resign?
Yes, you can withdraw pension fund after resignation, if your total service is less than 10 years and you remain unemployed for at least two months without joining another job, you are eligible to withdraw your EPFO pension fund.
2. How does EPF differ from life insurance?
EPF focuses on long-term retirement savings through regular contributions, while life insurance, in exchange for premium aim to provide financial security to the dependents in case of the life assured’s death
3. Can I withdraw 100% of my pension fund?
Full PF withdrawal is allowed only on retirement or an extensive unemployment period. After becoming unemployed, 75% can be withdrawn in the first month3. The balance 25% can only be withdrawn after 12 months of unemployment, and pension withdrawal is allowed only after 36 months3.
4. What is Form 10C for PF withdrawal?
Form 10C is used to claim withdrawal of the EPF pension contribution or to obtain a scheme certificate when an employee exits service before becoming eligible for a monthly pension under the Employees’ Pension Scheme (EPS)8.

changes.word_original: EPFO Pension Fund Withdrawal
changes.pdf_final: E Pension Scheme Withdrawal

changes.word_original: But EPFO pension fund withdrawal is often misunderstood; many salaried employees assume their pension can be easily accessed whenever they want, but in reality, pension withdrawals are governed by rigid eligibility rules and specific criteria.
changes.pdf_final: But EPFO pension fund withdrawal is often misunderstood; many salaried employees might think that premature withdrawals are allowed.
However, in reality, pension withdrawals are governed by specific eligibility rules and criteria.

changes.word_original: The Employee Pension Scheme, called EPS in short, was launched in the year 1995 under the aegis of the Employees' Provident Fund Organisation to provide salaried employees with a reliable pension after their retirement.
changes.pdf_final: The Employee Pension Scheme, called EPS in short, was launched in the year 1995 under the aegis of the Employees' Provident Fund Organisation to provide salaried employees with a reliable pension after their retirement1.

changes.word_original: From your employer's contribution, 8.33% (up to ₹15,000) goes to the EPS, and the rest goes to the EPF.
changes.pdf_final: From your employer's contribution, 8.33% (up to ₹15,000) goes to the EPS, and the rest goes to the EPF1.

changes.word_original: If you earn less than ₹15,000 a month, it's mandatory for you to have an EPF account.
changes.pdf_final: If you earn less than ₹15,000 a month, it's mandatory for you to have an EPF account1.

changes.word_original: The monthly pension is calculated as: (Pensionable Salary × Pensionable Service) ÷ 70
changes.pdf_final: The monthly pension is calculated as: (Pensionable Salary × Pensionable Service) ÷ 702

changes.word_original: This is the same for everyone who qualifies for EPS.
Pensionable salary is the average monthly salary from the last five years (60 months) before leaving the Employees’ Pension Scheme.
changes.pdf_final: Pensionable salary is the average monthly salary from the last five years (60 months) before leaving the Employees’ Pension Scheme2.

changes.word_original: The Supreme Court made this decision on November 4, 2022, changing the old rule.
changes.pdf_final: Previously, the pensionable salary was figured out using the average salary from the last year (12 months).

changes.word_original: Before, the pensionable salary was figured out using the average salary from the last year (12 months).
changes.pdf_final: Now, the average of the last five years is used to determine the amount of pension2.

changes.word_original: Now, the average of the last five years is used to determine the amount of pension.
Also, if an employee retires at 58 and has worked for over 20 years, two extra years are added to their pensionable service when calculating the pension.
changes.pdf_final: Also, if an employee retires at 58 and has worked for over 20 years, two extra years are added to their pensionable service when calculating the pension2.

changes.word_original: The online process is simple, but you'll need to link your Aadhaar card with your UAN first.
changes.pdf_final: The online process is simple, but you'll need to link your Aadhaar card with your UAN first1.

changes.word_original: Steps to Withdraw Pension Contribution Offline
changes.pdf_final: Steps to Withdraw Pension Contribution Offline1

changes.word_original: Step 1: Head to the EPFO website, find ‘Services,’ and then click ‘For Employees.’
changes.pdf_final: ● Step 1: Head to the EPFO website, find ‘Services,’ and then click ‘For Employees.’ ● Step 2: Select ‘Know the Claim Status.’ ● Step 3: You’ll be sent to the member passbook area. ● Step 4: Enter your UAN, password, and the captcha to log in. ● Step 5: Click ‘View Claim Status’ to see how your claim is coming along.

changes.word_original: Step 2: Select ‘Know the Claim Status.’

changes.word_original: Step 1: Sign in to the UAN Member Portal with your UAN and password.

changes.word_original: Check your PF claim status on the  UMANG App.

changes.pdf_final: Check your PF claim status on the UMANG App.

changes.word_original: Step 1: Get the UMANG app from the Play Store or App Store.
changes.pdf_final: ● Step 1: Get the UMANG app from the Play Store or App Store. ● Step 2: Log in to your EPFO account with your mobile number, using either MPIN or OTP. ● Step 3: Once you’re in, go to ‘Employee Centric Services.’ ● Step 4: You’ll be taken to the EPFO portal main page again.

changes.word_original: Step 2: Log in to your EPFO account with your mobile number, using either MPIN or OTP.
changes.pdf_final: Here, pick ‘Employee Centric Services.’ ● Step 5: Hit ‘Track Claim’ to see the status of your EPF claim. ● Step 6: Your claim details will show up.

changes.word_original: If you lose your job, you can get 75% of your PF money right away, and the other 25% after a year.
changes.pdf_final: If you lose your job, you can get 75% of your PF money right away, and the other 25% after a year3.

changes.word_original: There are also a few special situations where you can withdraw the full amount if you have worked for at least 1 year, i.e. 12 months.
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: If your workplace closes for more than 15 days, employees are jobless without pay.
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: If you haven't received your salary for over two months straight.
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: You can withdraw 100% of your share with interest, but the reason for non-payment could be anything other than a strike.
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: Partial EPF withdrawal is allowed only for specific purposes, and each purpose has defined limits and service requirements.
changes.pdf_final: Partial EPF withdrawal is allowed only for specific purposes, and each purpose has defined limits and service requirements4.

changes.word_original: To get your pension under the EPF Act, there's a set process you need to follow after retirement.
changes.pdf_final: To get your pension under EPS , there's a set process you need to follow after retirement.

changes.word_original: You can start getting your pension at the age of 50 or later.
If you've worked for at least 10 years, you can even withdraw earlier in case of emergency, but keep in mind that it will reduce your pension amount.
changes.pdf_final: You can start getting your pension at the age of 50 or later and have worked at least 10

changes.word_original: It goes down by 4% each year until you hit 50.
changes.pdf_final: years, you can even get pension early in case of emergency, but keep in mind that it will reduce your pension amount.

changes.word_original: If you've worked for over six months but less than ten years, you can still take out your pension money in case you are jobless for two months.
changes.pdf_final: It goes down by 4% each year until you hit 50. 2.

changes.word_original: If you retire at 58 but haven't worked the required 10 years, you won't get a monthly pension.
changes.pdf_final: If you've worked for over six months but less than ten years and have been unemployed for
2 months or more, you can withdraw your pension contribution. 3.
If you retire at 58 but haven't worked for the required 10 years, you won't get a monthly
pension.

changes.word_original: Eligibility to Withdraw EPF Completely After Retirement
changes.pdf_final: Eligibility to Withdraw EPF Completely After Retirement5

changes.word_original: Once your KYC is done, you can withdraw and move your PF money online without needing your employer to get involved.
changes.pdf_final: Once your KYC is done, you can withdraw your PF money online without much hassle5 .

changes.word_original: Claims are often turned down or delayed if KYC details are missing or not confirmed.
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: You can withdraw up to 90% of your EPF contribution if you want to repay an existing home loan.
changes.pdf_final: You can withdraw up to 90% of your EPF contribution if you want to repay an existing home loan subject to the conditions prescribed by EPFO.

changes.word_original: EPFO permits partial access to funds only for specific needs such as medical emergencies, education, marriage, or housing loans, etc., but you also must fulfil certain service year requirements before making a claim.
changes.pdf_final: EPFO permits partial withdrawals or advances only for specific needs such as medical emergencies, education, marriage, or housing loans, etc., but you also must fulfil certain service year requirements before making a claim.

changes.word_original: Unlike withdrawal, transferring PF does not attract any tax or TDS and helps maintain continuity of service, which is important for claiming full tax exemption on future EPF withdrawals after five years.
changes.pdf_final: Unlike withdrawal, transferring PF does not attract any tax or TDS and helps maintain continuity of service, which is important for claiming full tax exemption on future EPF
withdrawals after five years.

changes.word_original: Online EPF withdrawal has made the claim process significantly faster by removing paperwork and reducing repeated reliance on employer approvals for KYC-compliant UANs.
changes.pdf_final: Online EPF withdrawal has made the claim process significantly faster .

changes.word_original: EPFO is also working on enabling withdrawals through UPI using the BHIM app, where members are expected to view their total balance, eligible withdrawal amount, and the mandatory 25% minimum balance, with an initial transaction cap of ₹25,000.
changes.pdf_final: EPFO is also working on enabling withdrawals through UPI using the BHIM app, where members are expected to view their total balance, eligible withdrawal amount, and the mandatory 25% minimum balance, with an initial transaction cap of ₹25,0006.

changes.word_original: Eligible members can now withdraw up to 75% of their PF balance at any time without furnishing any documents, using the online portal.
changes.pdf_final: EPF members can now withdraw up to 75% of their PF balance at any time without furnishing any documents, using the online portal7.

changes.word_original: To protect the long-term retirement corpus, EPFO mandates that 25% of the PF balance remain in the account at any time.
changes.pdf_final: To protect the long-term retirement corpus, EPFO mandates that 25% of the PF balance remain in the account at any time7.

changes.word_original: Anytime Anywhere Access
Earlier, the process of withdrawal was complex, and it took several days for employers to actually receive the money.
changes.pdf_final: Anytime Anywhere Access7

changes.word_original: But now, with the integration of ATM and UPI in EPF 3.0, employees will be able to withdraw their PF money anywhere and anytime they want
changes.pdf_final: With the rollout of EPF 3.0, the integration of ATM and UPI is expected to significantly improve access to provident fund withdrawals.

Once implemented, employees will be able to withdraw their PF money instantly, offering employees easier and faster access to their PF money through ATMs and UPI, once the feature becomes operational.
This, however, is subject to specific terms and conditions.

changes.word_original: Members can now manage withdrawals, transfers, and claim tracking with just a few clicks anywhere, anytime.
changes.pdf_final: Members can now manage withdrawals, transfers, and claim tracking with just a few clicks .

changes.word_original: EPFO pension withdrawal is governed by strict eligibility rules based on age, years of service, and employment status, and cannot be accessed freely at any time.
The Employees’ Pension Scheme (EPS) pension amount is calculated using a standard formula that considers pensionable salary (average of last 5 years) and pensionable service.
changes.pdf_final: EPFO pension withdrawal is governed by strict eligibility rules based on age, years of service,

changes.word_original: Online pension withdrawal has simplified the process, provided Aadhaar is linked with UAN and KYC is complete.
changes.pdf_final: and employment status, and cannot be accessed freely at any time.

changes.word_original: Tax implications depend on years of continuous service, with withdrawals after five years generally being tax-exempt and early withdrawals potentially taxable.
Partial withdrawals and advance options are allowed only for specific purposes and under defined conditions to protect long-term retirement savings.
changes.pdf_final: The Employees’ Pension Scheme (EPS) pension amount is calculated using a standard

changes.word_original: While EPFO pension provides post-retirement income support, having life insurance is important to ensure the overall protection of dependents

and KYC is complete. 4.

generally being tax-exempt and early withdrawals potentially taxable3. 5.

changes.word_original: Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance can help shield your family from the financial shocks of unfortunate events.
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: Yes, you withdraw pension fund after resignation, but only if your total service is less than 10 years and you remain unemployed for at least two months without joining another job, you are eligible to withdraw your EPFO pension fund.
changes.pdf_final: Yes, you can withdraw pension fund after resignation, if your total service is less than 10 years and you remain unemployed for at least two months without joining another job, you are eligible to withdraw your EPFO pension fund.

changes.word_original: EPF focuses on long-term retirement savings through regular contributions, while life insurance, in exchange for premium aim to provide financial security to the dependents in case of the member’s death
changes.pdf_final: EPF focuses on long-term retirement savings through regular contributions, while life insurance, in exchange for premium aim to provide financial security to the dependents in case of the life assured’s death

changes.word_original: After becoming unemployed, 75% can be withdrawn in the first month.
changes.pdf_final: After becoming unemployed, 75% can be withdrawn in the first month3.

changes.word_original: The balance 25% can only be withdrawn after 12 months of unemployment, and pension withdrawal is allowed only after 36 months.
changes.pdf_final: The balance 25% can only be withdrawn after 12 months of unemployment, and pension withdrawal is allowed only after 36 months3.

changes.word_original: Form 10C is used to claim withdrawal of the EPF pension contribution or to obtain a scheme certificate when an employee exits service before becoming eligible for a monthly pension under the Employees’ Pension Scheme (EPS).
changes.pdf_final: Form 10C is used to claim withdrawal of the EPF pension contribution or to obtain a scheme certificate when an employee exits service before becoming eligible for a monthly pension under the Employees’ Pension Scheme (EPS)8.
